key_points: Graves Hall is the clear top choice for freshmen
Swanson is older but still considered decent housing
FLP students live on Graves top floor with FLP roommates only
key_quotes: you will want graves, im a freshman currently and graves is so nice
Swanson isnt bad, and people like it because its a little more social, but graves is the best
Two rooms share a bathroom, so four students share one bathroom

key_points: Parties exist but are strictly off-campus, not on university grounds
30-40% of campus in Greek life; sorority not necessary at smaller school
Social life requires effort — uninvolved students find it boring
key_quotes: Plenty of parties. But they will be off campus.
if you're not involved it can be pretty boring I will say, as someone who wasn't super involved on campus
I've had a great time here, no regrets about missing out on social life

key_points: Block 16 and Coneflower are student favorites near campus
On-campus options include Starbucks, McDonald's, Subway nearby
Skutt dining hall sub shop gets specific positive mentions
key_quotes: Block 16, coneflower, Mula, early bird, tap house are some Creighton favorites
Coneflower! Thank me later!
Astute Coffee is local and on the first level of The Atlas apartments across the pedestrian bridge

key_points: No direct student complaints or praise about mental health services found
Transferring out considered twice by at least one student due to fit issues
Disability services mentioned as a concern but no specific feedback given
key_quotes: I thought about transferring out of Creighton at least twice during my college career
When it comes to a disability, of course the school [has services]
There are so many opportunities to meet new people. Almost impossible not to find a community.

key_points: Merit aid is generous — students report $20K-$31K per year awards
At least 30% of student body receives non-need merit aid
After aid, costs can still hit ~$35K/yr — debate vs. state school value
key_quotes: Worth it to go to creighton w 31K merit aid a year?
my local state school would be about 13K a year with minimal aid so I really don't know if this is worth it
Creighton's $20K scholarship makes it much cheaper than WVU

key_points: Two required theology courses but taught secularly, not as religious doctrine
Credit transfer out is a real problem — nearly none transfer to other schools
Pre-med PPSP program is competitive and still relatively new (3-4 years old)
key_quotes: I learned that almost none of my credits would've transferred over
You have to take a few theology classes but they are taught in a secular lens
Taking more than 18 credits costs about $1500 per extra credit and needs advisor approval

key_points: Athletics admin drew criticism for intentionally cutting staff at games
$300M 'Fly Together' expansion prioritizes athletics infrastructure
Revenue sharing with athletes is forcing budget cuts across athletic programs
key_quotes: He intentionally reduced staff tonight at both the volleyball and soccer games
More athletics funding. That's the spirit.
The athletic department has to start revenue share with athletes this year, which means they have to reduce expenses elsewhere

key_points: Omaha is consistently praised as an underrated, livable city
Campus is described as beautiful with strong school spirit
Winter is genuinely harsh — wind chills well below freezing, 5+ inches of snow common
key_quotes: Omaha is really unrated as a city
The current temperature in Omaha is 27 degrees, with wind chill making it feel like 15. There's 5 inches or so of snow on the ground.
Nice size, beautiful campus and a lot of school spirit (fantastic basketball and soccer teams)

key_points: Creighton law school reputation questioned vs. Nebraska law for local careers
Business school tied to Berkshire Hathaway connection in Omaha noted
Creighton name carries more weight nationally than students often expect
key_quotes: Not worth it [Creighton Law]. Quality in professors have gone down within the last 5 years. Going to Nebraska law will give you the same or better education for lower price.
Creighton is a bigger name than most realize. It's become a way for me to know people wherever I go.
Worth it if you want to stay and live in Omaha

key_points: Strong merit aid can make Creighton competitive with state school pricing
Without aid, $35K+/yr is hard to justify over in-state options
Value highly dependent on major — health professions seen as stronger ROI
key_quotes: my local state school would be about 13K a year... I really don't know if this is worth it
Depends on the degree. What are you planning to study?
You'll get in with scholarship money for sure

key_points: Welcoming, Jesuit-rooted culture without heavy religious pressure on students
Leans politically liberal for a Catholic school; student body more conservative
Smaller size means tight community but requires initiative to stay engaged
key_quotes: Creighton is a very accepting school, and the culture makes it feel welcoming
It's as liberal as a Catholic university is going to get. Ironically, I found the student population to be quite conservative.
It's as religious as you want it to be short of two theology class requirements

key_points: **Academics:** Nearly zero credits transfer out — you're locked in once enrolled
**Law School:** Professor quality reportedly declining; Nebraska Law offers better value locally
**Administration:** Athletic director actively cutting staff and dismissing successful programs
key_quotes: I learned that almost none of my credits would've transferred over
Not worth it. Quality in professors have gone down within the last 5 years
From a 3rd party quote he thinks men's soccer is a dying sport. Easily the most successful sports program for the university and he could not give two shits.

key_points: PPSP direct medical school program is a rare guaranteed med school pathway
Berkshire Hathaway HQ in Omaha gives business students unique local access
Omaha performing arts, local food scene, and city culture regularly surprise students
key_quotes: accepted to creighton with 31k a year and ppsp med program!
Omaha is really unrated as a city
Block 16, coneflower, Mula, early bird, tap house are some Creighton favorites
